programmes.show_description: The British Olympic Synchronised Swimming team stay at the Solana, and are booked to perform in the pool. Their coach is not impressed with the hotel or Joyce's efforts, however. S5 E5
When Arthur offers Terry the chance to make the easiest money he'll ever earn, it is clear there are going to be complications. S1 E2: Bury My Half at Waltham Green
Alf is feeling the effects of the demon drink. Else is unsure whether it was the single glass of port or the sausages that made him ill. S5 E6: Party Night
Robin's brother Norman comes to stay for two days and takes a shine to Chrissy; when the three of them go out to dinner, Robin gets food poisoning. S6 E5: Mum Always Liked You Best
Amy's suspicions are further aroused by David and Linda's midnight swim, and Robert also suspects that a romance is in the air. S1 E5: Bedroom Farce

In tribute to Ozzy Osbourne. The family's numerous dogs have been excreting in the house and ruining valuable furniture; the worst dog is Lola, a bulldog which belongs to Jack. S1 E2: Bark at the Moon
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E6: X Marks The Spot
Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E7: Something in the Night
This programme offers much rare footage made on the railways of the southeast of England between the 1930s and the 1960s.
The home of a great night's sleep. Every body. Every bedroom. Every budget. Teleshopping.
Holiday inspiration. Discover the perfect holiday for you, featuring the hottest destinations and exclusive deals to make your next holiday the best one. Teleshopping.
Jet Hawk is cordless, rechargeable and turns any water source into a powerful pressure washer. Call or visit highstreettv.com for more details. Teleshopping.

A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!

Auntie Vera has got problems with her hips and her defective lifts and tells Sharon and Tracey she is moving in - there's only one room available, though. S12 E5: Tonight I'll Be Staying Here With You
The crew of the television holiday programme 'Wish You Were Here' - along with its presenter Judith Chalmers - are at the hotel. S3 E3: Close Up
It's the autumn of 1908 as Sarah returns while James and Elizabeth are helping the poor at a refuge; soon brought back to the house, Sarah stirs up trouble for all. S1 E10: A Voice From The Past
Freddie is arrested for stealing a dustcart, Grandad is taken to hospital, Billy quarrels with Julie and Jack is nearly seduced by Carmen. S3 E7
Harold has arranged a poker game with a few friends; Albert is morally outraged by the idea of gambling, but he sees that Harold is being cheated by his friends and decides to help. S2 E6: Full House
May is keen to make a career move to a bigger station, but both Jack and Cecil are set against her going and both try to apply for a transfer to be near her. S1 E8: Job Opportunity
Rigsby returns from a Spanish holiday to find Alan has let out the two spare rooms; he's happy Miss Jones has returned but less enthusiastic about a single mother and her baby. S3 E1: That's My Boy
Robin accompanies Chrissy to her nephew's christening while Jo runs out of excuses not to hear George sing in the British Legion's choir concert. S4 E5: The Tender Trap
When Yvonne lands her dream job up north, Gary feels he must finally choose between the two women he loves. S2 E5: Wish Me Luck
Jack is finding his feet and form again as social commentator Margaret Carter-Brown meets him and he shows her how the Depression affects families in Gallowshield. S4 E3: A Medal for the Argentine
Both Terry and Arthur are captivated by an Australian girl who claims to be searching for her long-lost boyfriend, but what is her real motive? S4 E6: If Money Be the Food of Love, Play On
John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny gets a divorce and a proposal on the same day. S2 E7: Goodbye Again
With Jo gone for the weekend, Chrissy frets over being alone in the flat with Robin. S1 E4: And Then There Were Two
Mrs Slocombe is stunned when her long-lost husband appears to have made enquiries about buying the hotel... especially since he's been missing since he went to a supermarket in 1942. S2 E3

Auntie Vera has got problems with her hips and her defective lifts and tells Sharon and Tracey she is moving in - there's only one room available, though. S12 E5: Tonight I'll Be Staying Here With You
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Harold has arranged a poker game with a few friends; Albert is morally outraged by the idea of gambling, but he sees that Harold is being cheated by his friends and decides to help. S2 E6: Full House
Classic sitcom about a miserly landlord. Rigsby faces the prospect of a lonely Christmas until Alan and Philip arrive unexpectedly. S2 E8: For the Man Who Has Everything (Christmas Special 1975)
When faced with a fight, Terry has a golden rule: 'Do unto others as they would do unto you - but do it first'. S2E12: Conduct Unbecoming
Terry leaves his fingerprints on a stolen lorry while doing a job for Arthur, then panics at the prospect of retribution. S7 E1: It's a Sorry Lorry Morrie
As Madge prepares for her marriage to Mohammed, Mick and Michael discover all is not what it seems with the wealthy Moroccan suitor. Donald and Jacqueline play detectives, searching for a mole. S5 E6
When people think Frank's won the lottery, he's not about to say otherwise, with endless free drinks coming his way. S5 E4

Mildred is upset that she has not received an invitation to Norman and Chrissy's wedding, but George has hidden it from her because he wants to go to a darts match. S6 E7: Another Bride, Another Groom
Rigsby is given some advice from Alan and Philip - as well as some tablets - to calm his nerves in his amorous pursuit of Miss Jones. S1 E4: Charisma
As Madge prepares for her marriage to Mohammed, Mick and Michael discover all is not what it seems with the wealthy Moroccan suitor. Donald and Jacqueline play detectives, searching for a mole. S5 E6
Arthur gets involved with a small-time crook who manages to steal a suitcase filled with money - and soon realises that the notes are suspect. S1 E3: The Smaller They Are...
Alf is concerned to find that his neighbour's house has been sold to the Council for social housing. S5 E7
Chrissy and Norman have been dating for some weeks to the annoyance of Robin, who goes on a disastrous double date with Larry. S6 E6: Fire Down Below
David refuses to go with Amy to visit the ruins by saying that he already booked a court to play tennis with Linda; Robert still does not suspect, but Amy finally sees the evidence. S1 E6: Forty Love
In tribute to Ozzy Osbourne. Ozzy releases his first solo album in six years and takes photos with fans including an entire squad of security guards. S1 E3: Like Father Like Daughter
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E8: Lucky For Some
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E1: For Better, For Worse
The last steam-worked main lines on the Southern Region, from Waterloo to Salisbury and Weymouth, are recalled on this all-colour programme which features cine film made between 1958 and 1967.

A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
A surprise visitor in the form of attractive Emma turns up at Tracey's house - Tracey is welcoming, Dorien is shocked to the core, and Travis is over the moon. S12 E6: Abandoned Love
David and Linda use the situation to their advantage when Amy and Robert can't stand the accident-prone Neville. S3 E4: The Go-Between
October 1908: there is a thief in the house, but who is the culprit? Is it Sarah, a visiting friend of James, or perhaps something more organised than just petty theft? S1 E11: The Swedish Tiger
Mr Boswell's court date clashes with the day Billy and Julie have secretly decided to get married. S4 E1
Harold falls for a wealthy married woman while out on his rounds; Albert is unimpressed and tries to talk Harold out of making a fool of himself. S2 E7: Is That Your Horse Outside?
Jack and May have problems with their teenage daughter, and it's dance night and Ethel is excited about hoping to find love at last. S1 E9: Sleeping Around
Rigsby attempts to have himself cast as male lead in a play written by effeminate lodger Hilary when he discovers that Ruth is playing the role of the love interest. S3 E2: Stage Struck
George has been claiming for a non-existent son for the past 19 years in order to fiddle his tax returns, but he is in danger of being caught when a tax inspector visits. S4 E6: My Son, My Son
The hardships of war life begin to take toll on Gary and he contemplates returning to the present. Reg is disappointed when Gary fails to turn up for fire-watching duty. S2 E6: As You Wave Me Goodbye
Jack is back to ducking and diving and buying bankrupt stock from the South Americans for Sarah to sell; he has also found romance with Margaret the well-bred socialist. S4 E4: Flies & Spiders
When a rock star fakes his own death, Arthur finds himself in possession of a valuable master tape that has every chance of becoming a 'posthumous' hit. S4 E7: A Star is Gorn
Bittersweet sitcom. Ex-fiances Penny and Vince meet unexpectedly in Paris after two years - will that certain something still be there? S3 E1: Paris
Robin and Chrissy return home to find their front door open and the rent money that was on the sideboard missing. S1 E5: It's Only Money
Captain Peacock discovers a dead cat hidden behind one of the walls - after the staff get rid of it, they find out that it was put in the hotel to cancel out bad luck. S2 E4
A surprise visitor in the form of attractive Emma turns up at Tracey's house - Tracey is welcoming, Dorien is shocked to the core, and Travis is over the moon. S12 E6: Abandoned Love
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Harold falls for a wealthy married woman while out on his rounds; Albert is unimpressed and tries to talk Harold out of making a fool of himself. S2 E7: Is That Your Horse Outside?
Rigsby returns from a Spanish holiday to find Alan has let out the two spare rooms; he's happy Miss Jones has returned but less enthusiastic about a single mother and her baby. S3 E1: That's My Boy

The death of disreputable old Uncle Jacob is received by the Collier family with a mixture of regret and relief as the funeral gives Bob an insight into his future. S2 E13: The Shape of Things to Come
Arthur attempts to do a deal with Tony Benson and his new nightclub. Terry helps Dave by looking after the Winchester Club. S7 E2: Days of Fines and Closures

Mick has bad news for the Garveys, as he reveals that all of their sunbed shops have burnt down. Joyce struggles for her fourth star, and discovers the true identity of the secret hotel assessor. S5E7
Shane falls for his perfect woman, but when she takes him back to her place, he's in for a surprise. S5 E5
The morning after a party, Chrissy and Jo find Robin asleep in their bath and ask him to become their third roommate. S1 E1: Three's a Crowd
Mr Spooner annoys the household by playing his radio loudly - Rigsby is annoyed, but he finds it difficult to pluck up the courage to confront him about it. S1 E5: All Our Yesterdays
